data.title: Belgian court overrules Alstom's appeal of SNCB's pick for 1.7 billion euro trains deal
data.description:
By Alban Kacher

Belgium's top administrative court on Wednesday rejected Alstom's request to suspend national rail operator SNCB's decision to name CAF as the preferred supplier for its AM30 train contract.

"The Council of State estimates, after an interim review, that none of the criticisms made by the company challenging the legality of this appointment decision were serious," it said in a statement.

In April, the court suspended the order following Alstom's appeal against the decision.

The order plays a crucial role in determining the future of Alstom's Bruges facility, which faces the threat of restructuring and possible closure.

SNCB's tender, the biggest the company has placed, consists of a first batch valued at 1.7 billion euros ($2 billion), with optional batches potentially bringing the total contract value to 5 billion euros.

Earlier this month, the Council rejected Siemens' separate appeal against SNCB's decision.

($1 = 0.8518 euros)

data.title: Lithium stocks light up as focus shifts to data
data.description:
Key points:
  • Major U.S. stock indexes essentially unchanged
  • Energy tops sector gains, real estate falls the most
  • Euro STOXX 600 index down ~0.3%
  • Dollar jumps, gold near record high; crude climbs ~1.5%, bitcoin rises
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield ticks up to ~4.14%

LITHIUM STOCKS LIGHT UP AS FOCUS SHIFTS TO DATA

U.S. stocks hovered near the flatline on Wednesday, lacking a fresh catalyst after all three major indexes snapped multi-day winning streaks in the previous session. Investors are now looking ahead to a heavy slate of economic data in the back half of the week.

After Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated caution over further rate cuts on Tuesday, focus will be on the GDP report, weekly jobless claims, consumer confidence, PCE data spread across Thursday and Friday.

U.S.-listed shares of Lithium Americas soared more than 90% after a report said the Trump administration is seeking up to a 10% stake in the miner. Lithium Americas confirmed it is in talks with the U.S. Department of Energy and General Motors , its partner in the Thacker Pass JV, regarding the first draw on the $2.26 billion loan, lifting GM shares by 2%.

Other lithium stocks rallied in sympathy: Albemarle jumped 5.4% to lead gains on the S&P 500 , while Sigma Lithium up 9.6%, U.S.-listed shares of SQM added 3.5%.

In contrast, Freeport-McMoRan slumped 9.5% after the miner forecast lower consolidated sales for copper and gold in the third quarter.

Alibaba ADRs jumped 7.7% after announcing a tie-up with Nvidia, global data center expansion plans and new artificial intelligence products. Other China stocks got a boost too.

Among S&P sectors, the energy index led gains and was trading near a six-month highs. Real estate dropped the most, down 0.4%.

data.title: Lithium Americas Shares Jump Amid Report of Possible U.S. Government Stake
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Lithium Americas shares soared Wednesday morning after a Reuters report said the U.S. government is in talks to take a stake as big as 10% in the Canadian mining company.

Shares trading in Toronto rose over 81% to 7.65 Canadian dollars ($5.53).

The move by the Trump administration would be its latest effort to leverage loans made under the Biden administration into a stake in a company, like it did with tech giant Intel, as it looks to encourage U.S. manufacturing capacity.

Reuters reported the news late Tuesday.

Lithium Americas is developing a Nevada lithium mine called Thacker Pass along with General Motors, backed by a $2.26 billion loan from the U.S. Energy Department.

The company was awarded the loan under the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing program to help build out the processing facilities at Thacker Pass, which is among the largest known lithium deposits in the U.S.

But the project has come under scrutiny over its viability and U.S. administration officials have raised concerns about the company's ability to repay the loan given sluggish lithium prices.

Earlier on Wednesday, Lithium Americas said it was in talks with the Department of Energy and its partner General Motors over the terms of its $2.26 billion government loan for the project.

The talks are meant to cover requirements that must be met before the financing can be used, details of the loan itself, as well as new requests from the DOE that could add conditions or alter its terms, the Canadian miner said. GM, a partner in the project, is participating in the discussions.

Lithium is a critical mineral used in electric vehicle batteries, smartphones and renewable energy storage. In recent years it has become a priority for the U.S. as it seeks to cut reliance on foreign suppliers.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Cintas Corp reports results for the quarter ended August 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cintas Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.20​​ per share for the quarter ended August 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.10. The mean expectation of sixteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.19 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.13 to $1.23 per share.

  • Revenue rose 8.7% to $2.72 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $2.70 billion.

  • Cintas Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.20​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $491.14 million.

  • Cintas Corp shares had fallen by 10.0% this quarter and gained 9.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

data.title: DXY Rises Toward 3-Week High
data.description:
The dollar index rose above 97.8 on Wednesday, approaching a three-week high, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell highlighted growing risks for both inflation and employment.

Powell warned of “two-sided risks” with inflation tilted upward due to tariffs, and employment pressured by weakening job creation and tighter labor supply amid stricter immigration policies.

He offered no signal on whether he supports a rate cut at the Fed’s October meeting but stressed that decisions are not politically driven.

Powell reiterated that tariffs’ inflationary impact should be temporary, while the labor market slowdown warrants caution.

Fed officials remain divided, with projections indicating two more rate cuts this year, although some argue for fewer, while others advocate for aggressive easing.

Markets now expect quarter-point cuts at the October and December meetings, with attention turning to upcoming labor data and Friday’s PCE inflation report.

data.title: XP Is Maintained at Buy by Citigroup
ICE Canola on the Rise
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange maintained their positive momentum Wednesday morning as rising comparable oils and a weaker Canadian dollar supported prices.

Chicago soyoil, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were higher. Crude oil also made gains due to supply issues and a drop in U.S. inventories.

The loonie was down nearly three-tenths of a U.S. cent compared with Tuesday's close.

Parts of central Saskatchewan and central Alberta will see rain Wednesday, while southern areas of the Prairies will be sunny. Alberta will see normal temperatures, Manitoba will be warmer than usual, and the high in Regina will be 30 degrees Celsius later in the day.

Manitoba's harvest was 56% complete as of Sept. 21, with the province's canola crop 45% combined.

data.title: Amazon's Anthropic Partnership Will Drive Significant AWS Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Anthropic will drive significant revenue growth for Amazon Web Services, say Wells Fargo analysts, who upgrade Amazon to overweight from equal weight. The analysts increase their AWS revenue estimates by 3% for 2026 and 7% for 2027, largely due to higher expectations for revenue from Amazon and Anthropic's Project Rainier compute build-out. Meanwhile, market share losses for AWS will likely improve in 2026 and beyond as AWS accelerates, alleviating competitive concerns, the analysts say. Amazon is up 0.7% in early trading. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Bloom Energy falls after Jefferies downgrades stock to 'underperform'
data.description:
** Shares of fuel-cell maker Bloom Energy down 12% at $67.9

** Jefferies downgrades Bloom Energy to "underperform" from "hold", but raises price target to $31 from $24

** The new price target still represents a 60% downside to the stock's last close

** Brokerage sees limited visibility beyond 2026 and early signs of over-exuberance, suggesting downside risks outweigh further upside at current levels

** "BE's association with Oracle has sparked excitement, especially with OpenAI's Stargate project boosting ORCL's backlog... we estimate this deal could be in the 15–50 megawatt range — good, but not great," brokerage says

** BE's fuel cell delivery is fast, but infrastructure delays can slow deployment - Jefferies

** New PT reflects "mark-to-market of multiples and higher volume", brokerage says, adding that it has "no confidence in the volume estimates for 2027"

** BofA also raises PT to $24 from $21, implying an about 70% downside to stock's last close

** 13 of 25 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 9 "hold" and 3 "sell" or lower; median PT is $44.5 - data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's moves, stock has nearly tripled YTD

data.title: Ukraine's Kyivstar gets 'outperform' rating as Oppenheimer starts coverage
data.description:
** Oppenheimer initiates coverage on Ukrainian mobile operator Kyivstar with "outperform" rating

** Brokerage sets $20 PT, implying a near 70% upside to stock's last close

** Says KYIV has about 50% market share in wireless subscribers market despite low ARPU (average revenue per user), expects strong ARPU growth

** Digital services and fiber broadband, which currently add 11% and 5% respectively to revenue, can triple in the next seven years alongside a 10%-15% growth in revenue per year, brokerage says

** Post-war rebuild in Ukraine could add 200-400 basis points to KYIV's growth rate - Oppenheimer

** KYIV shares reversed premarket gains to fall 0.8% to $11.67

** Including session's move, KYIV down nearly 19% from its IPO last month
